summary: Deep research + adversarial verification for P2 gap partition 1 (21 models)
logs: research: 22 models researched, 0 rejected as unconfirmable
verify: 22 models confirmed, 0 rejected by verification

models.name: GLM-130B
models.license: Custom GLM-130B Model License (non-commercial research; code Apache-2.0)
models.archDisplay: Dense bidirectional Transformer (GLM objective)
models.note: GPT-3-scale 130B bilingual (EN/ZH) dense model; open code, weights and training logs (ICLR 2023).

results.notes: Zero-shot accuracy; beats GPT-3 175B, OPT-175B, BLOOM-176B

models.verificationNotes: Fully confirmed via arXiv:2210.02414 (ar5iv HTML). LAMBADA zero-shot 80.2 and MMLU 5-shot 44.8 both appear verbatim in the paper. MODEL_LICENSE on GitHub confirms non-commercial-research-only restriction plus China-specific clauses; code is Apache-2.0. Paper confirms 130B bilingual EN/ZH dense GLM-objective model, ICLR 2023 acceptance, training completed July 2022 with public release/report August 2022. No changes made.

models.name: CodeGeeX (13B)
models.license: Apache-2.0 (code); custom Model License for weights (free research use; commercial use after registration)
models.archDisplay: Dense autoregressive Transformer (GPT-style, trained w/ GLM recipe)
models.note: 13B multilingual code model covering 23 programming languages; introduced HumanEval-X benchmark (KDD 2023).
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed via GitHub README (13B params, Apache-2.0 code license + separate MODEL_LICENSE for weights, max sequence length 2,048 = ctxK 2) and arXiv:2303.17568 HTML (Table 5: HumanEval-X Python pass@1 = 22.89% exact match). Paper accepted at KDD 2023 (Aug 2023, Long Beach) but model itself pretrained/released by Zhipu in Sept 2022, consistent with record. No changes made.

models.name: ChatGLM-6B
models.license: Apache-2.0 (code); ChatGLM-6B Model License for weights (free for research; free commercial use after registration)
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer (GLM, prefix-LM decoder)
models.note: Pivotal open bilingual (EN/ZH) dialogue model runnable on consumer GPUs via INT4 (6GB); China's early open ChatGPT-style release.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed exactly: release date March 14, 2023 per the official ChatGLM technical report arXiv:2406.12793 ("went live on chatglm.cn" that date). GitHub README confirms VRAM table FP16=13GB / INT4=6GB verbatim, and ~2048 token effective context. License wording ("completely open for academic research, free commercial use after questionnaire") confirmed verbatim in README, refining the raw MODEL_LICENSE text which itself doesn't mention registration. C-Eval score 38.9 confirmed verbatim in Table 3 of arXiv:2305.08322 (ar5iv). No changes made.

models.name: ChatGLM2-6B
models.license: Apache-2.0 (code); ChatGLM2-6B Model License for weights (free for research; free commercial use after registration)
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer (GLM, Multi-Query Attention, FlashAttention)
models.note: Second-gen 6B chat model; FlashAttention-based context extended to 32K, +23% MMLU / +571% GSM8K over gen 1, faster INT4 inference.
models.tpsNote: 44.62 tok/s generating 2K tokens, measured on A100-SXM-80G (official repo); no RTX 4090 llama.cpp figure found
models.verificationNotes: Fully confirmed. Release date June 25, 2023 confirmed by multiple independent sources (MarkTechPost coverage dated June 28 2023 referencing the June 25 GitHub announcement) and the official ChatGLM technical report. GitHub README_EN.md table confirms MMLU 45.46 / C-Eval 50.1 / GSM8K 28.05 / BBH 30.00 verbatim, and VRAM table confirms FP16=13.1GB / INT4=5.5GB verbatim (encoding-2048-tokens column). Context extension 2K->32K (base) with 8K dialogue training confirmed. No changes made.

models.name: CogVLM-17B
models.license: Apache-2.0 (code); CogVLM Model License for weights (non-commercial use / commercial by agreement)
models.archDisplay: Dense dual-tower vision-language Transformer (trainable visual expert modules)
models.note: Open VLM (10B vision expert + 7B Vicuna-based LM) with SOTA-level results on 10 cross-modal benchmarks at 490x490 resolution.
models.verificationNotes: Corrected releaseDate from the unconfirmed exact day 2023-10-05 to month-precision 2023-10: GitHub commit history (checked via API) shows the earliest real commits Sept 28-Oct 9, 2023 with no activity specifically on Oct 5; independent Chinese-language press coverage (Jiqizhixin, Tencent News) appeared Oct 12-15, 2023; the arXiv paper wasn't submitted until Nov 6, 2023 and HF weights uploaded Nov 16, 2023. Params (17B = ~10B vision expert + 7B Vicuna LM), Apache-2.0 code license + separate Model License, and INT4 VRAM ~11GB on RTX 3090 all confirmed verbatim in README. VQAv2 84.38 / GQA 82.87 corroborated by an independent web search but I could not directly open the full PDF (10MB size limit) to see the table myself, so treat with slightly lower confidence than other rows. Removed vramFp16Gb (record's 29GB) since README only gives system-level guidance (1xA100-80G or 2xRTX3090-24G) and I found no single verified per-model FP16 VRAM figure to corroborate 29GB specifically.

models.name: ChatGLM3-6B
models.license: Apache-2.0 (code); ChatGLM3-6B Model License for weights (free for research; free commercial use after registration)
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer (GLM, Multi-Query Attention, SwiGLU, 28 layers)
models.note: Third-gen 6B model adding native function calling, code interpreter and agent tool use; strongest sub-10B base model on its release benchmarks.
models.verificationNotes: Release date Oct 27, 2023 confirmed exactly via the official ChatGLM technical report arXiv:2406.12793 (its release table lists 2023-10-27). All six benchmark scores (MMLU 61.4, C-Eval 69.0, GSM8K 72.3, MATH 25.7, BBH 66.1, MBPP 52.4) confirmed verbatim in the GitHub README_en.md evaluation table for the Base checkpoint. Function calling/code interpreter/agent capabilities confirmed verbatim ("natively supports tool invocation, code execution, and Agent tasks"). 8K context confirmed. Corrected vramFp16Gb from 14.13 to 13 (README states "running requires about 13GB of VRAM" in two places; no source supports 14.13). Adjusted vramQ4Gb from 7 to 6: no official quantization table was found for ChatGLM3-6B specifically (unlike ChatGLM/ChatGLM2 which have explicit tables), so this is an estimate aligned with the confirmed same-scale ChatGLM-6B INT4 figure (6GB) rather than a directly sourced number — flagged as lower confidence than the other spec fields.

models.name: GLM-4
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer (undisclosed size; architecture details not published)
models.note: Original closed flagship unveiled at Zhipu DevDay, 128K context + 'All Tools' agent, positioned near GPT-4; since retired from the API in favor of GLM-4-Long/Flash and the open GLM-4.x/GLM-5 lines.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Zhipu AI's first-ever DevDay (Jan 2024) launch of GLM-4, 128K context + All Tools agent, positioned near GPT-4 (MarkTechPost, Maginative). All 5 result rows re-fetched and confirmed verbatim in Table 2 of arxiv.org/html/2406.12793v1 (GLM-4 0520 checkpoint vs GPT-4-0314). paramsB/archClass correctly left undisclosed/null — no primary source discloses size. No changes made.

models.name: GLM-4-9B-Chat
models.license: GLM-4 License (custom, free for research & most commercial use)
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (GLM-4 architecture)
models.note: Open 9B GLM-4 checkpoint (co-developed by Zhipu AI/THUDM Tsinghua) beating Llama-3-8B on semantics/math/code/reasoning; 26 languages, 128K context (separate 1M variant exists).
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real. Release date 2024-06-05 verified via GitHub zai-org/GLM-4 file literally named README_20240605.md. HF safetensors metadata shows 9,399,951,392 params (9.4B, rounds to 9 — matches). config.json seq_length=131072 confirms 128K context. License tag is 'license:other' (custom GLM-4 license) — matches. All 6 result rows (MMLU 72.4, GSM8K 79.6, MATH 50.6, HumanEval 71.8, IFEval 69.0, C-Eval 75.6) re-confirmed verbatim on the HF README. No changes made.

models.name: GLM-4V-9B
models.license: GLM-4 License (custom, free for research & most commercial use)
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer + ViT vision encoder (GLM-4V, CogVLM-style)
models.note: Open multimodal GLM-4 variant adding 1120x1120 Chinese/English image understanding; claimed to beat GPT-4-turbo-2024-04-09, Gemini 1.0 Pro, Qwen-VL-Max and Claude 3 Opus on multimodal benchmarks.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real, same 2024-06-05 release batch as GLM-4-9B-Chat (GitHub README_20240605.md). Notably the paramsB:14 in the source record (despite the '9B' model name) is CORRECT — verified via HF API safetensors metadata showing 13,906,320,928 total params (13.9B, rounds to 14) because of the added ViT vision encoder. config.json seq_length=8192 confirms 8K context (matches ctxK:8, unusually short vs the text-only 9B's 128K). All 4 result rows (MMBench-EN 81.1, MMBench-CN 79.4, AI2D 81.1, OCRBench 786) re-confirmed verbatim on the HF README. No changes made.

models.name: GLM-4-Plus
models.archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (assumed dense transformer)
models.note: Closed flagship unveiled at KDD 2024; official docs claim AlignBench 83.2 (99% of GPT-4o) and MMLU 86.8 (98% of GPT-4o), i.e. near-parity language/instruction-following/long-text performance.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: release date 2024-08-29 at KDD 2024 corroborated by multiple independent news sources (aibase.com). Both result rows re-fetched from docs.bigmodel.cn and confirmed verbatim: AlignBench 83.2 vs GPT-4o 83.8, MMLU 86.8 vs GPT-4o 88.7. paramsB correctly left null/undisclosed — no primary source discloses size. No changes made.

models.name: GLM-4-32B-0414
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer, 32B, pretrained on 15T tokens
models.note: MIT-licensed 32B base pretrained on 15T tokens (incl. reasoning-heavy synthetic data); competitive with GPT-4o/DeepSeek-V3 on IFEval, BFCL-v3 function calling and TAU-Bench agent tasks at a fraction of the size. Separate 128K-context variant also released.
models.tpsNote: Third-party calculators estimate ~30 tok/s at Q4 on RTX 4090 (extrapolated, not a measured llama.cpp benchmark).
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real. Release date 2025-04-14 corroborated by PRNewswire/Yahoo Finance ('Z.ai Unveils New GLM Open-Source Models...') and Synced (2025-04-16 article reporting on the launch two days prior) — note some auto-summarized tool output misread the year as 2024, but independent news coverage confirms 2025. MIT license confirmed via HF API license tag. Exact param count via HF safetensors API is 32,566,081,536 (32.57B); kept as 32 to match the model's own name/marketing (HF UI itself displays a rounded '33B' — both are consistent with the true 32.57B figure, not an error). config.json max_position_embeddings=32768 confirms 32K native context. All 3 result rows (IFEval 87.6, BFCL-v3 69.6, TAU-Bench-retail 68.7) re-confirmed verbatim on the HF README.

models.name: GLM-Z1-32B-0414
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (reasoning-tuned GLM-4-32B-0414 via cold-start SFT + RL)
models.note: Open reasoning model built on GLM-4-32B-0414 via cold-start plus extended RL; evaluated on AIME24/25, MATH-500, GPQA and LiveCodeBench, marketed as much faster than DeepSeek-R1 (vendor claims up to 200 tok/s vs 671B R1).
models.tpsNote: Vendor claims ~200 tok/s on Zhipu's own MaaS platform; not an independently measured RTX 4090 llama.cpp benchmark.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real, released the same day (2025-04-14) as GLM-4-32B-0414 in the same Z.ai open-source batch ('base, reasoning, and rumination models... all under the MIT license' per PRNewswire announcement). MIT license and 'developed based on GLM-4-32B-0414 through cold start and extended reinforcement learning' description confirmed on the HF model card. HF safetensors API shows identical 32,566,081,536 params (32.57B, matches the 32B base it was tuned from). config.json max_position_embeddings=32768 confirms 32K context. Original record had zero result rows, so none needed to be dropped — nothing to verify there. No changes made.

models.name: Step-1
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer, 100B+ params, bilingual (Chinese/English)
models.note: StepFun's debut 100B+-parameter bilingual LLM, publicly launched March 2024; offered via API in 8K/32K/128K/256K context variants (step-1-8k/32k/128k/256k per StepFun's model list docs), since superseded by the Step-3.x series and dropped from current pricing.
models.verificationNotes: Existence and March 2024 launch confirmed via Wikipedia/AI Wiki/nextomoro descriptions of StepFun's 100B+-parameter Step-1. Confirmed step-1-8k/32k/128k/256k context variants genuinely exist in StepFun's official API model-list docs. No result rows were provided to verify. paramsB/date/openness/license left unchanged as they matched independent descriptions.

models.name: Step-2
models.archDisplay: Trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts (first from a Chinese firm)
models.note: First trillion-parameter MoE LLM from a Chinese firm; previewed March 2024, officially launched July 2024 at WAIC; Step-2-16k ranked 5th globally (1st in China) on LiveBench in Nov 2024, behind only OpenAI and Google models.

results.notes: Global average; ranked 5th overall, 1st among Chinese models

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: MarkTechPost (2024-11-20) independently corroborates trillion-parameter MoE architecture and 16k context for Step-2-16k, and WAIC July 2024 launch (with March 2024 preview) matches Wikipedia/search consensus. The LiveBench 57.68 global-average score, 5th-globally/1st-in-China ranking is confirmed via indexed content of the exact cited lianpr.com URL (direct fetch returned 403 but the search engine's cached excerpt of that same URL states the 57.68 figure verbatim); a separate ICLR 2025 LiveBench paper reports a different snapshot score (55.1) for a later dated checkpoint (step-2-16k-202411), which is consistent with LiveBench scores drifting across evaluation rounds rather than contradicting the cited claim.

models.name: Step-1V
models.archDisplay: Dense multimodal transformer (LLM backbone + vision encoder), exact scale undisclosed
models.note: StepFun's first publicly released multimodal (vision) model, adding image understanding atop the Step-1 text base in September 2024; since deprecated in favor of newer Step vision models.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: nextomoro and other search results independently confirm Step-1V launched September 2024 as StepFun's first public multimodal model, with Step-Audio/Step-Video following in Oct/Nov 2024. Confirmed a step-1v-32k variant genuinely exists in StepFun's official model-list docs, supporting the ctxK=32 value. No result rows were provided to verify. Params left null (undisclosed) as given.

models.name: Step-3
models.archDisplay: MoE — 48 experts (3 routed + 1 shared per token), Multi-Matrix Factorization Attention (MFA) + Attention-FFN Disaggregation (AFD)
models.note: Open-source 321B MoE (38B active) multimodal reasoning VLM, Apache-2.0, co-designed with MFA/AFD for cost-effective decoding.
models.tpsNote: 321B total params must stay resident even with 38B active — infeasible on a single consumer GPU; needs multi-GPU/datacenter serving.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real and independently corroborated: 321B total/38B active MoE, Apache-2.0 code+weights, and July 31, 2025 release date all confirmed via HuggingFace (stepfun-ai/step3), GitHub (stepfun-ai/Step3), and the arXiv system paper (2507.19427, submitted 2025-07-25). Context length 64K (65536 tokens) confirmed via HF model card text. Pricing $0.57/$1.42 per 1M input/output tokens confirmed via independent pricing aggregator search. DROPPED 4 of 5 result rows (gpqa 67.1, aime 73, livecodebench 83.7, math-vision 64.2): the official capability-benchmark table on stepfun's research page exists only as an embedded image (figures/step3_bmk.jpeg per the GitHub README, not machine-readable text), and repeated independent fetch attempts of that same page/image produced mutually contradictory numbers for GPQA-Diamond (read as both 70.0 and 73.0), AIME25 (read as both 67.1 and 82.9), and LiveCodeBench (read as both 83.7 and 67.1) -- none could be literally confirmed with confidence, so per instructions they were dropped. Kept only MMMU=74.2, which was consistently and independently reproduced across three separate fetches/searches (direct page fetch, a third-party SiliconFlow blog post in narrative text, and general web search synthesis).

models.name: Cerebras-GPT 13B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer, GPT-3 style (40 layers, d_model 5120, 40 heads)
models.note: Largest of Cerebras' seven-model, Chinchilla-optimal, fully-open GPT family trained entirely on CS-2 wafer-scale hardware.
models.verificationNotes: Fully confirmed: March 28, 2023 release date corroborated by Forbes, MarkTechPost, and Cerebras' own press release/X announcement (7-model family, 111M-13B, Chinchilla-optimal, trained on CS-2). Apache-2.0 and 13B params confirmed on HF model card. All 4 result rows (hellaswag 51.3, arc-challenge 36.7, winogrande 64.6, lambada 69.6) verified as literally present, matching exactly, in the HF model card's 0-shot results table (which also lists PIQA 76.6, not included in the input record).

models.name: BTLM-3B-8K
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer, GPT-2 style + SwiGLU + ALiBi + muP (32 layers, d_model 2560)
models.note: Bittensor Language Model: 2.6B (marketed as 3B) with 7B-class quality and 8K context, trained on 627B tokens of SlimPajama, trained on the Condor Galaxy 1 (CG-1) supercomputer (Cerebras/G42 partnership).
models.tpsNote: Cerebras reports the model fits in under 3GB of memory at 4-bit precision for mobile/edge deployment.
models.verificationNotes: CORRECTED releaseDate: input record cited 2023-09-20 (the arXiv paper's submission date), but the model was actually publicly released earlier -- HuggingFace commit history shows weights uploaded ~2023-07-24, and Cerebras/Opentensor's own announcement says BTLM 'went live on Bittensor' July 27, 2023; downgraded to month precision (2023-07) since sources disagree on the exact day. Confirmed exact param count is 2.6B (not marketed '3B') via the paper. All 5 result rows (hellaswag 69.8, arc-challenge 37.6, winogrande 65.8, mmlu 28.1, humaneval 9.9) verified as literally present in Table 2 of the paper via the ar5iv HTML rendering (a text-accurate mirror of the arXiv LaTeX source) -- notably an initial web-search-synthesized read of the same paper produced shuffled/wrong numbers (e.g. arc-challenge misread as 65.8), which the direct ar5iv HTML fetch corrected. Apache-2.0 and 8192 context confirmed via HF model card.

models.name: Poro 34B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer, BLOOM architecture + ALiBi (54 layers, d_model 7168, 56 heads)
models.note: Fully open Apache-2.0 Finnish/English/code model trained on 1T tokens on the LUMI supercomputer; pivotal European low-resource open LLM.
models.tpsNote: Q4 GGUF (~20GB) fits on a single 24GB RTX 4090 with limited headroom for context.

results.notes: Finnish FIN-bench average; FinGPT 13B scored 48.92 for comparison
Average of HumanEval and MBPP code accuracy as reported in paper

models.verificationNotes: Fully confirmed by fetching the actual arXiv PDF (2404.01856, Luukkonen et al., "Poro 34B and the Blessing of Multilinguality") and the HF model card. Architecture (54 layers, d_model 7168, 56 heads, BLOOM+ALiBi), 34.2B params, Apache-2.0 license, and 2048 sequence length all match Table 5 of the paper exactly. All 8 benchmark rows verified literally against Tables 6, 7, and 8 of the paper, including the FinGPT-13B=48.92 comparison note and the HumanEval/MBPP average (37.20/47.40 → 41.80).

models.name: Viking 7B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer, LLaMA-style + RoPE + FlashAttention, standard multi-head attention (32 layers, d_model 4096, 32 heads)
models.note: First open LLM covering all Nordic languages plus English and code; base checkpoint of the 7B/13B/33B Viking family trained on LUMI.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed via HF model card (7.55B params, 32 layers, d_model 4096, RoPE+FlashAttention, Apache-2.0, 4096 seq length, Finnish/English/Swedish/Danish/Norwegian/Icelandic+code, 2T training tokens) and tech.eu article (release date 2024-05-15). CORRECTED: the original record's architecture claim of "grouped-query attention" is false — fetched the model's config.json directly and found num_attention_heads == num_key_value_heads == 32, i.e. standard multi-head attention, not GQA. archDisplay updated accordingly; no other changes.

models.name: Grok-3
models.archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (exact expert configuration undisclosed by xAI)
models.note: xAI's reasoning-focused flagship trained on the Colossus supercluster (~10x Grok-2 compute); default standard (non-Think) serving mode. Deprecated May 2026, retiring Aug 2026.
models.verificationNotes: Existence, release date (livestream Feb 17 2025, corroborated by TechCrunch/CBS News), MoE architecture (undisclosed exact config, corroborated by multiple secondary sources referencing Colossus 10x compute), context window (131,072 tokens ≈128K per prompthub.us), and deprecation timeline (deprecated May 15 2026, retires Aug 15 2026 per docs.x.ai migration notice, no longer listed on current docs.x.ai/developers/models) all confirmed. Pricing $3/$15 per 1M tokens confirmed via direct fetch of prompthub.us (quoted verbatim) and pricepertoken.com. AIME=52 confirmed literally on deeplearning.ai The Batch article. Arena Elo=1402 confirmed literally on chatbase.co/blog/grok-3.

models.name: Grok-3 (Think)
models.archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (exact expert configuration undisclosed by xAI)
models.note: Same Grok-3 weights with extended test-time reasoning (Think/Big Brain modes) enabled via API parameter, at highest benchmarked test-time compute (cons@64).

results.notes: AIME 2025, cons@64, Think mode — highest test-time compute benchmarked

models.verificationNotes: Same underlying model/release as Grok-3 standard (see that record's notes for existence/date/pricing/deprecation verification). All three Think-mode benchmark rows confirmed literally on chatbase.co/blog/grok-3: AIME 2025 cons@64 = 93.3%, GPQA Diamond = 84.6%, LiveCodeBench = 79.4%.
